pages.text:
__Friday May 28th__

     We set off as planned at 2, but had to loop back because Simon had forgotten his telephone. We delivered lights to Strat ford & reached __Chipping Campden__ at 3.30., where Simon is chatting to William Welsch.
     We just reached __Malvern__ at 6.35, in time for the 7 Summer Concert. Charles stood for applause, with 3 other brass players at the end of the William Tell Overture. John was in every piece. A lovely Mozart chamber music piece.
__Saturday 29th__

     We slept outside No 7. Then met up with Jane & Harry & Koroku for the service at Malvern Theatre & prize giving also at Malvern Theatre. We had a good sermon from the & Dean Emeritus of Wells Cathedral: "When God works he works to the sound of Bach, when he eavesdrops he listens to the angels who listen to Mozart."

service to the world without arrogance or egotism. The choir sang "I'd like to teach the world to sing." because the former head of Coca Cola for Brit. & Ireland gave the prizes, Penny Hughes. She became head at 33, then left after 10 years to have children.
     We had lunch at SH, then set off at 2 in the van, arriving at __Ninas__ at 6. On the way we stopped for tea & saw William & Bryony & Thomas drive past in William's & Bryony's new orange Vw camping van. They had telephoned us at __Welshpool__ to ask about shopping.
     We had chicken salad & stodge for supper. The midges were bad. Thomas lit a fire. Bryony found an orchid in the meadow.
__Sunday 30th May__

     It rained heavily in the night & the day took a while to warm up. After breakfast Thomas set off for a walk & went 11 miles, only returning at 7.25. for supper. The rest of us read & chatted & Bryony made coffee. Simon & Eve took the wild flower book into the meadow & tried to identify flowers. There were blue dragon[flies?]

After lunch we went off on a lovely 2hr walk, up the hill behind the campsite. Tonic had trouble climbing stone wall stiles & was good about not chasing sheep. When we got back his front leg got paralysed & he had spasms. Since then he has been fine.
   Simon & Charles & William cooked 18 lamb chops & sausages on the bbq. Then we lit Thomas' "fire", inside an old tyre, & cooked smores. John & Charles kept putting grass on, black smuts.
   __Monday May 31st__
   It was cold in the night. After breakfast Thomas, William & Bryony left in the orange van. We layed about. I read in the meadow.
   After lunch we set off for Harlech, but as we left through the campground gates the water bracket fell off twice.
   We parked in the Castle carpark at Harlech & wandered around the town buying coffee, bread, milk & icecreams. Then we drove back via Llanbedr. There we stopped too, but there was nothing to buy. The "Queen Victoria" pub grows

[Printed map of North Wales glued in — decorative illustrated map titled "NORTH WALES" showing regions including Wirral, Chester, Wrexham, Rhyl, Colwyn Bay, Llandudno, Conwy, Bangor, Caernarfon, Harlech, Barmouth, Cardigan Bay, Caernarfon Bay, Holyhead, Anglesey, and many other place names]

its own vegetables. Back at the campsite we had tea. Then it started to rain & we read & read. Charles & Simon managed to light the fire & the boys sat next to it after supper.
